name: Common Cold
description: A viral infection of the upper respiratory tract causing nasal congestion, sneezing, and mild fever. In Ayurveda it is primarily a Kapha-Vata disorder caused by weakened immunity and exposure to cold.
diet_plan: Light, warm, easily digestible foods; avoid dairy and cold items.
foods_to_take: Warm water with ginger and lemon, tulsi (holy basil) tea, garlic, honey, warm soups, light khichdi, pomegranate juice
foods_to_avoid: Cold drinks, ice cream, heavy dairy (milk, cheese, yogurt), fried foods, sweets, bananas, cucumbers, cold salads
lifestyle_routine: Steam inhalation with eucalyptus oil, rest adequately, avoid day sleeping, keep body warm, apply warm mustard oil on chest, do nasya with warm ghee drops
recommended_exercises: Kapalabhati Pranayama, Anulom Vilom (Alternate Nostril Breathing), gentle Sun Salutation

name: Vata-Type Arthritis
description: Joint disorder caused by aggravated Vata dosha, characterized by cracking, popping, dry joints with no swelling. Pain worsens with movement and strenuous exercise.
diet_plan: Warm, moist, easily digestible, oily foods; follow Vata-pacifying diet.
foods_to_take: Warm soups, ghee, sesame seeds, warm milk with turmeric, ginger tea, castor oil in ginger tea, well-cooked grains, root vegetables
foods_to_avoid: Cold food and drinks, raw salads, beans, barley, corn, tomatoes, potatoes, eggplant (nightshades), dry crackers, carbonated drinks
lifestyle_routine: Apply Mahanarayan oil on affected joint followed by moist heat soak; warm sesame oil foot and scalp massage at bedtime; avoid jogging and jumping; keep joints warm; warm water enema to cleanse colon
recommended_exercises: Forward Bend (gentle), Chest-Knee pose, Maha Mudra, Half Bridge pose, Anulom Vilom Pranayama

name: Pitta-Type Arthritis
description: Inflammatory arthritis driven by excess Pitta dosha. Joints appear red, hot, and swollen and are painful even without movement.
diet_plan: Cooling, light foods; follow Pitta-pacifying diet strictly.
foods_to_take: Coconut water, cucumber, coriander tea, cooling fruits (sweet varieties), ghee, aloe vera gel, mung dal
foods_to_avoid: Hot spicy foods, pickles, spinach, tomatoes, red meat, fermented foods, alcohol, sour citrus fruits, fried foods
lifestyle_routine: Apply cool castor oil or coconut oil to painful areas; apply sandalwood paste on inflamed joint; apply ice bag when joint is hot; avoid sun exposure and high temperatures; practice Shitali Pranayama
recommended_exercises: Boat, Bow, Camel, Cow, Locust poses; Moon Salutation; Shitali Pranayama (cooling breath)

name: Bronchial Asthma
description: A chronic respiratory condition characterized by sudden attacks of wheezing, shortness of breath, and chest tightness. Primarily a Kapha disorder with Vata involvement in the respiratory tract.
diet_plan: Light, warm, dry foods; avoid cold, heavy, and mucus-forming foods.
foods_to_take: Ginger tea with honey, warm water, mustard seed tea, tulsi tea, light soups, pomegranate juice, warm spiced foods, cloves
foods_to_avoid: All dairy products (milk, cheese, yogurt), fermented foods, salty items, cucumber, tuna fish, mushrooms, peanuts, walnuts, yeast-containing foods, cold beverages
lifestyle_routine: Avoid known allergens (dust, mold, chemicals); apply warm mustard oil on chest; use warm ghee drops in nostrils (nasya) if sinus infection is cause; avoid damp environments; sleep with head elevated; avoid smoking
recommended_exercises: Bow pose, Cobra pose, Vajrasana (sitting), Shoulder Stand, Plow pose, Kapalabhati Pranayama, Anulom Vilom

name: Vata-Type Anemia
description: Anemia with Vata imbalance, characterized by thin build, dry rough scaly skin, cracking joints, breathlessness, constipation, and possibly tarry black stools.
diet_plan: Iron-rich, nourishing, warm, oily foods to build blood and pacify Vata.
foods_to_take: Beets, carrots, grapes, raisins, currants, figs, dates, pomegranate juice, cranberry juice, beet-carrot juice with cumin, spinach, chard, fresh green vegetables, warm milk with Tikta Ghrita
foods_to_avoid: Cold foods, dry foods, raw vegetables, excessive beans, barley, excessive fasting, carbonated drinks
lifestyle_routine: Take Tikta Ghrita (bitter ghee) before meals; take Gandharva Haritaki at bedtime; eat yogurt with turmeric on empty stomach (morning and afternoon only, not after sunset); avoid heavy exertion
recommended_exercises: Gentle yoga, Anulom Vilom Pranayama, So-Hum Meditation, gentle walking

name: Constipation
description: Difficulty in passing stools or infrequent bowel movements, primarily a Vata disorder caused by dry, cold, or irregular diet, stress, and reduced agni (digestive fire).
diet_plan: High-fiber, warm, oily, and moist foods; follow Vata-pacifying diet.
foods_to_take: Warm milk with ghee at bedtime, flaxseed tea, prunes, raisins, peaches, pineapple juice, apple (raw, peeled), oatmeal, wheat bran, soaked figs, warm water throughout the day
foods_to_avoid: Dry foods (chips, crackers), cold foods, excessive raw vegetables, beans, barley, refined flour products, carbonated drinks, excess caffeine
lifestyle_routine: Drink 4-5 glasses of warm water daily; take Triphala (1 tsp) with warm water at night; practice nauli (abdominal exercise); regular bowel timing each morning; avoid suppressing urge to defecate; reduce stress
recommended_exercises: Sun Salutation (12 cycles daily), Chest-Knee pose, Leg Lift, Nauli (abdominal churning), 30-minute daily walk or light jog

name: Fever
description: Elevation of body temperature as a response to infection or inflammation. Ayurveda classifies fever by dosha type. Chronic fever involves deep Pitta imbalance with systemic toxin accumulation.
diet_plan: Light, cooling, easily digestible foods; fasting or semi-fasting encouraged during acute fever.
foods_to_take: Tulsi (holy basil) tea with black pepper and honey, warm water, light mung dal soup, pomegranate juice, coconut water, barley water, maha sudarshan churna in warm water
foods_to_avoid: Heavy meat, fried foods, dairy, sweets, spicy and hot foods, alcohol, raw cold vegetables, excess oil
lifestyle_routine: Rest completely; avoid travel and exercise; apply damp cool cloth on forehead for high fever; for pitta fever-induced convulsions use grated onion compress on forehead and belly; take Maha Sudarshan Churna
recommended_exercises: Complete rest during acute fever; gentle Pranayama only after recovery; Shavasana (Corpse pose) for relaxation

name: Vata-Type Headache
description: Throbbing, pulsating headache originating in the occipital (back) region of the head, often associated with neck tension, constipation, dehydration, and sciatica. Worsens with movement.
diet_plan: Follow Vata-balancing diet with warm, nourishing, easy-to-digest foods.
foods_to_take: Warm soups, ghee, sesame seeds, warm milk, ginger tea, homemade oral rehydration (sugar, salt, lime), warm grains, root vegetables
foods_to_avoid: Cold and iced drinks, raw salads, dry foods, carbonated beverages, excessive caffeine, beans, fermented foods
lifestyle_routine: Apply nutmeg paste on forehead; warm sesame oil scalp and foot massage at night; warm water enema to relieve constipation; take Triphala to detoxify colon; Ghee Nasya (3-5 drops warm ghee in each nostril); neck and shoulder oil massage; correct dehydration with dextrosaline
recommended_exercises: Anulom Vilom Pranayama, gentle Shavasana, gentle neck rolls, So-Hum Meditation

name: Pitta-Type Headache
description: Burning, shooting, or piercing headache starting at the temples, often behind the eyes. Worsened by bright light, heat, spicy food, and sour fruits. May be associated with nausea and dizziness.
diet_plan: Follow Pitta-pacifying diet with cooling foods.
foods_to_take: Sweet fruits, ice cream (occasionally), cumin-coriander tea, coconut water, rose water, aloe vera juice, cool (not cold) milk, cucumber
foods_to_avoid: Sour fruits, pickles, hot spicy foods, alcohol, red meat, fermented foods, tomatoes, salt in excess
lifestyle_routine: Apply sandalwood powder paste on forehead and temples; apply Brahmi oil or Bhringaraj oil on scalp and feet at night; avoid walking in the sun without a hat; Ghee Nasya with warm ghee in nostrils; keep environment cool and dimly lit
recommended_exercises: Shitali Pranayama (cooling breath), Moon Salutation, Shavasana, Chandra Bheda Pranayama

name: Hypertension
description: Abnormally elevated blood pressure resulting from increased blood viscosity (Kapha), constriction of vessels (Vata), or forceful blood flow (Pitta). Long-term hypertension risks heart disease and stroke.
diet_plan: Cooling, light, low-salt diet; avoid fatty and spicy foods.
foods_to_take: Mango juice followed by warm spiced milk, orange-coconut water mix, peach juice with cardamom and coriander, watermelon with cardamom, cucumber raita, mung dal soup with cumin and turmeric, honey water with apple cider vinegar
foods_to_avoid: Salt (excess), fatty fried foods, hot spicy food, red meat, alcohol, coffee, canned foods, processed foods, heavy cheeses
lifestyle_routine: Avoid working in hot sun; practice meditation daily (So-Hum, Transcendental); drink magnetic water twice daily; limit stressful activities; no public speaking when BP is elevated; monitor BP regularly with doctor supervision
recommended_exercises: Gentle yoga, Shavasana, Pranayama (Anulom Vilom), daily walking, So-Hum Meditation, no vigorous exertion

name: Indigestion
description: Weak digestive fire (Agni) leading to incomplete digestion, bloating, gas, heaviness, and discomfort. Usually caused by overeating, incompatible foods, irregular eating habits, and emotional stress.
diet_plan: Light, warm, freshly cooked foods; follow disciplined meal timings.
foods_to_take: Ginger tea, onion juice with honey and black pepper, pineapple juice with ginger and black pepper, lime juice in warm water with baking soda (acute), lassi (diluted yogurt with cumin and ginger), bay leaf tea
foods_to_avoid: Overeating, cold and iced drinks (especially during meals), leftover food, fried foods, heavy meats, cheese, raw onions, beans, incompatible food combinations (e.g., milk with fish)
lifestyle_routine: Eat only when genuinely hungry; fill stomach 1/3 food, 1/3 water, 1/3 empty; chew food thoroughly; no emotional eating; 2-3 meals daily without snacking; short walk after meals; practice Nauli; occasional fasting
recommended_exercises: Leg Lifts, Peacock pose, Nauli (abdominal churning), Breath of Fire (Kapalabhati) Pranayama, 10-minute post-meal walk

name: Insomnia
description: Inability to fall or stay asleep, primarily a Vata disorder affecting the nervous system. Often linked to stress, anxiety, constipation, depression, or overstimulation of the senses.
diet_plan: Warm, grounding, and nourishing Vata-pacifying foods, especially at dinner.
foods_to_take: Warm milk with nutmeg and cardamom at bedtime, cherries (10-20 daily), tomato juice with natural sugar and nutmeg (late afternoon), chamomile tea at bedtime, garlic milk, warm almond milk
foods_to_avoid: Stimulants (coffee, tea, cola), alcohol, spicy foods at night, heavy dinner, excessive screen time before bed, cold foods and drinks in the evening
lifestyle_routine: Warm sesame oil (or Brahmi oil) massage on scalp and soles of feet before bed; apply nutmeg-ghee paste around eyes and forehead; establish regular sleep schedule; reduce screen time after sunset; practice evening meditation; warm bath before bed
recommended_exercises: So-Hum Meditation, Shavasana, Yoga Nidra, gentle Anulom Vilom Pranayama before bed, gentle evening walk

name: Kapha-Type Anemia
description: Anemia presenting with edema (swelling), cold and clammy skin with a shiny appearance due to stretched skin. Indicates Kapha dominance with fluid retention and sluggish metabolism.
diet_plan: Light, dry, warm, iron-rich foods; follow Kapha-reducing diet.
foods_to_take: Beets, carrots, pomegranate juice, cranberry juice, figs, raisins, spinach and other green leafy vegetables, spiced warm teas, honey
foods_to_avoid: Dairy products, cold food and drink, heavy meats, wheat, sweets (except honey), watermelon, cucumber, excess salt
lifestyle_routine: Take Punarnava-Gokshura-Kutki herbal mixture twice daily with warm water or honey; eat yogurt with turmeric only at noon; avoid day sleeping; regular physical activity to combat sluggishness
recommended_exercises: Vigorous yoga, Sun Salutation (12 cycles), Kapalabhati Pranayama, brisk walking, Surya Namaskar

name: Kapha-Type Arthritis
description: Joint stiffness with swelling and cold, clammy sensation. Pain is greater in the morning and diminishes with gentle movement. Joints may fill with fluid (effusion).
diet_plan: Light, dry, warm foods; follow Kapha-reducing diet.
foods_to_take: Ginger tea, warm spiced soups, light grains, honey, pungent spices (black pepper, ginger, turmeric), bitter vegetables
foods_to_avoid: All dairy products, cold drinks, heavy oily foods, sweets, wheat, meat, excess salt, cold salads
lifestyle_routine: Apply Vacha (calamus root) paste on affected joint; for effusion use Punarnava-Ginger powder paste; take Punarnava Guggulu tablets (1 tablet, 3x/day); keep joints moving gently; avoid cold and damp environments
recommended_exercises: Tree pose, Triangle pose, Forward Bend, Spinal Twist, Surya Namaskar, Kapalabhati Pranayama, gentle walking

name: Dandruff
description: Dry, flaky scalp condition caused by insufficient blood supply to the scalp, excess Vata dosha, Vitamin B-6 deficiency, or fungal infection of the scalp.
diet_plan: Nourishing Vata-pacifying foods rich in B vitamins, zinc, and healthy fats.
foods_to_take: Sesame seeds, flaxseeds, coconut oil (in food), green leafy vegetables, warm milk, almonds, figs, warm soups, ginger tea
foods_to_avoid: Dry and processed foods, excess caffeine, cold drinks, refined flour, sugary foods, excessive spicy food
lifestyle_routine: Daily scalp massage with Neem oil (in sesame oil base) to improve circulation; apply egg-white with lime juice to hair for 30 minutes then wash with neem soap; avoid harsh chemical shampoos; keep scalp moisturized; manage stress
recommended_exercises: Inverted yoga poses (Shoulder Stand, Headstand with supervision), Anulom Vilom Pranayama, Sun Salutation, Scalp massage

name: Acne
description: Acne is the result of high Pitta moving under the skin and breaking out in pimples. The possible Pitta-provoking causes are numerous — emotional stress, premenstrual hormonal changes, exposure to chemicals or too much sunlight, and bacterial infection. Because acne is a Pitta condition, the root cause is excess heat in the body that rises to the surface of the skin.
diet_plan: Follow the Pitta-pacifying diet; favor bland, light foods.
foods_to_take: Rice, oatmeal, applesauce, coconut water, cucumber, sweet fruits, aloe vera juice (½ cup twice daily), cumin-coriander-fennel tea after meals
foods_to_avoid: Spicy and fermented foods, salt, fried foods, citrus fruits, chocolate, alcohol, refined sugar, sour fruits
lifestyle_routine: Apply sandalwood-turmeric paste in goat's milk on face (leaves yellow tint up to 5 days); wash face daily with chickpea flour paste; apply melon pulp on skin at bedtime and leave overnight; drink ½ cup aloe vera juice twice daily; take Amalaki ½–1 tsp at bedtime to keep colon clean; avoid chemicals, excess sunlight, and emotional stress; avoid looking in the mirror and feeling bad about the acne; practice visualization — close eyes and visualize skin clearing up
recommended_exercises: Lion Pose; Moon Salutation; Left Nostril breathing (Moon Breath) for 5–10 minutes daily to reduce Pitta; rub warm palms over face for 2 minutes to relax facial muscles and increase blood supply

name: Rashes and Hives
description: Skin rashes and hives indicate excess Pitta or heat in the body. The Ayurvedic approach is to provide a cooling effect from both the inside and outside. Whatever the cause of the rash — whether allergies, an insect bite, or something else — the underlying mechanism is aggravated Pitta penetrating through the capillaries and creating heat-based skin reactions.
diet_plan: Cooling Pitta-pacifying diet; blood-purifying foods.
foods_to_take: Coconut water, melon, coriander-cumin tea in hot milk (once or twice daily), cilantro juice, pomegranate juice, sweet fruits
foods_to_avoid: Hot spicy foods, fermented foods, sour citrus fruits, alcohol, red meat, hot beverages
lifestyle_routine: Apply fresh cilantro juice immediately on skin; apply coconut water directly on rash; rub melon rind (white part of watermelon) on affected skin; apply sandalwood-turmeric paste in goat's milk on affected area; avoid all synthetic fibers (polyester, rayon) — wear only cotton clothing; avoid allergens; apply neem oil on exposed skin to minimize allergen contact; avoid excess sun exposure
recommended_exercises: Moon Salutation; Shitali Pranayama (cooling breath); Shavasana; avoid Sun Salutation and heating practices

name: Dry Skin
description: Dry skin can have several causes. It may be due to a lack of sebaceous (oily) secretions, insufficient sweating, an excess of hot sharp Pitta, or too much Vata. External causes include sun, wind, hot dry air, excess washing, and excess use of soap. Dryness of the skin usually comes from within rather than from external causes, which is why moisturizing creams provide only temporary relief.
diet_plan: Oily, nourishing, Vata-pacifying diet; adequate healthy fats internally.
foods_to_take: Sesame oil in cooking, ghee, warm milk, almonds, coconut, avocado, warm soups, sweet fruits, root vegetables
foods_to_avoid: Dry and processed foods, excess caffeine, cold drinks, refined flour, excessive raw vegetables, alcohol
lifestyle_routine: Apply sesame oil (Vata type), sunflower or coconut oil (Pitta type), or corn oil (Kapha type) on skin daily; perform oil enema for internal lubrication — Week 1: daily, Week 2: every 2 days, Week 3: every 3 days, Week 4: once; apply fresh cherry pulp mask on face at night for 15 minutes then rinse; avoid harsh soaps and excess washing; protect skin from sun, wind, and hot dry air; treat from within — external creams give only temporary relief
recommended_exercises: Gentle yoga; Anulom Vilom Pranayama; gentle walking; avoid excessive sweating exercises that dehydrate the skin further

name: Eczema
description: Eczema is a Pitta-type allergic skin disorder. Pitta dosha is already present under the skin, and when the person comes in contact with an allergen such as chemicals, ragweed, or synthetic fibers, the Pitta penetrates through the capillaries due to its hot and sharp qualities and creates a rash, itching, hives, urticaria, or allergic dermatitis. Blood purification is the core of treatment.
diet_plan: Cooling anti-inflammatory Pitta-pacifying diet; blood-purifying foods.
foods_to_take: Coconut water, aloe vera juice, cucumber, coriander tea, bitter vegetables, cooling sweet fruits, pomegranate juice, cilantro juice
foods_to_avoid: Hot spicy foods, fermented foods, sour citrus, alcohol, red meat, dairy, synthetic fiber clothing, nightshade vegetables
lifestyle_routine: Apply neem oil or Tikta Ghrita (bitter ghee) directly on affected skin; take Manjistha-Neem blood-purifying formula ½ tsp 3x/day with warm water; avoid scratching; wear only cotton clothing and avoid synthetic fibers; consider blood donation (Rakta Moksha) before summer season to reduce systemic Pitta; avoid allergens; apply neem oil on exposed skin as protective barrier
recommended_exercises: Moon Salutation; Shitali Pranayama (cooling breath); cooling Pranayama; Alternate Nostril breathing; avoid heating and strenuous exercise

name: Athlete's Foot
description: People with Kapha-Pitta constitutions who sweat a lot are most prone to getting athlete's foot. This is an itchy, inflammatory fungal condition between the toes, often accompanied by sweating on the soles of the feet. The combination of excess moisture (Kapha) and heat (Pitta) creates the ideal environment for fungal growth.
diet_plan: Kapha-Pitta-pacifying diet; reduce sugar and dairy which feed fungal growth.
foods_to_take: Neem tea, bitter vegetables, garlic, ginger, coconut water, light foods, pomegranate juice, warm water
foods_to_avoid: Refined sugar, dairy products, fermented foods, alcohol, yeast-containing foods, refined flour, cold heavy foods
lifestyle_routine: Clean affected area with tea tree oil on cotton swab; apply aloe vera gel + ½ tsp turmeric twice daily for at least 2 weeks (will stain yellow — use old socks); alternatively wash feet with neem soap, dry thoroughly with hair dryer, then apply neem oil (¼ tsp) mixed with 10 drops tea tree oil; keep feet completely dry after washing; wear only cotton socks; change socks daily; expose feet to fresh air and sunlight daily; avoid fermented food and sugar
recommended_exercises: Foot yoga exercises; toe spreading; walking on grass (when infection has cleared); Anulom Vilom Pranayama; avoid activities causing excessive foot sweating during treatment

name: Baldness and Hair Loss
description: Hair loss is a subtle metabolic disorder. It may be related to disease, fungal infection on the scalp, or hormonal imbalance. A deficiency of calcium, magnesium, and zinc may affect hair nourishment enough to cause hair loss. According to Ayurveda, early hair loss is often related to body type and dosha balance. Pitta individuals are more likely to lose hair early, as excess Pitta in the sebaceous gland at the root of the hair, or folliculitis (inflammation of hair follicles), makes the person start losing hair.
diet_plan: Pitta-pacifying hair-nourishing diet rich in calcium, magnesium, and zinc.
foods_to_take: White sesame seeds (handful daily — 1200mg calcium), coconut, cooked apples, cabbage, white radish, daikon, dairy (if well digested), aloe vera juice (⅓ cup 3x/day for 3 months)
foods_to_avoid: Hot spicy foods, fermented foods, excess alcohol, tobacco, excessive sugar, sour foods, excess salt, red meat
lifestyle_routine: Massage scalp at bedtime with Brahmi oil or Bhringaraj oil to improve circulation at hair roots; rub coconut oil on scalp and soles of feet at bedtime (wear old socks, cover pillow with towel); massage hair with Vitamin E oil; take mineral supplements at bedtime (calcium 1200mg, magnesium 600mg, zinc 60mg); massage neck and shoulder muscles before showering to relieve stress contributing to hair loss; do simple neck exercises (turn head left 3x, right 3x, up 3x, down 3x, roll in circles 3x each direction); drink Jatamamsi-Brahmi Antistress Tea 2–3x/day; meditate regularly for stress reduction using Empty Bowl meditation
recommended_exercises: Shoulder Stand; Camel pose; Cobra pose; Cow pose — all help relieve neck tension and improve scalp circulation; Anulom Vilom Pranayama; Simhasana (Lion pose)

name: Diarrhea
description: Generally, diarrhea occurs when Agni (the digestive fire) becomes weak. As a result, absorption and assimilation become minimal, and undigested foodstuff gets eliminated as a liquid watery stool. To relieve diarrhea, Ayurveda aims to strengthen Agni and pacify whichever dosha — generally Pitta — is aggravated. Indigestion, nervousness, or eating wrong food combinations can also create diarrhea.
diet_plan: Pitta-pacifying diet; easily digestible binding foods; avoid spicy and fermented foods immediately.
foods_to_take: Cooked apple with ghee, cardamom and nutmeg; ripe banana with ghee; cooked basmati rice with yogurt and ghee; ginger powder with natural sugar; yogurt blended with grated ginger; cranberry juice or pomegranate juice
foods_to_avoid: Spicy foods, fermented foods, oily food, raw vegetables, excess fruit juice, cold drinks, alcohol, dairy in excess
lifestyle_routine: Immediately follow Pitta-pacifying diet; avoid dehydration — sip sugar-lime-salt water throughout the day (1 tsp sugar + 1 tsp lime juice + pinch salt per pint of water); take psyllium husks (sat isabgol) 1 tsp mixed into cup of fresh yogurt at bedtime for Pitta diarrhea; complete rest; do not travel or exert; consult doctor if diarrhea continues beyond 3 days; for babies — give cooked apple with ghee; feed whey to restore colon flora
recommended_exercises: Complete rest during acute diarrhea; Shavasana; Anulom Vilom Pranayama (gently); resume light yoga only after full recovery; no vigorous exercise until stools normalize

name: Gas and Flatulence
description: Every person at some time gets gases and disturbances in the colon. The colon is the main seat of Vata dosha, derived from ether and air. If Vata increases in the colon due to eating Vata-aggravating foods, cold weather, anxiety, or insomnia, gases may build up. Also, whenever we eat anything we swallow a small amount of air which increases Vata, and any food undergoes slight fermentation producing gases. These gases in the segmented colon create flatulence, distension, and discomfort.
diet_plan: Light, easily digestible, warm cooked foods; proper food combining; avoid gas-producing combinations.
foods_to_take: Ginger tea, warm water with asafetida (hing), cumin-fennel-celery seed mixture after meals, lemon juice with baking soda in cool water after meals, fresh ginger with lime juice after eating, light khichdi, buttermilk with cumin
foods_to_avoid: Beans and legumes (without proper preparation), raw vegetables, cold drinks, carbonated beverages, fried foods, excess bread, incompatible food combinations
lifestyle_routine: Take fresh ginger pulp with lime juice immediately after eating; take lemon juice + baking soda in cool water quickly after meals; chew roasted cumin-fennel-ajwan seeds ½–1 tsp after each meal with warm water; take charcoal tablets after lunch and dinner; take shankavati and lasunadivati herbal tablets at night for 5 days; take Triphala ½–1 tsp in hot water at bedtime; follow Vata-pacifying diet; avoid raw foods, cold food and drinks, most beans, and fermented foods; avoid eating when emotionally upset
recommended_exercises: Pavanamuktasana (Wind-Relieving pose); Leg Lift; gentle Spinal Twist; Nauli (abdominal churning); walking after meals

name: Hemorrhoids
description: According to Ayurveda, there are two basic kinds of hemorrhoids. Vata hemorrhoids are small, dry, and irregular in shape, rough and hard to the touch like raisins, and may be accompanied by fissures. Pitta-type hemorrhoids get red, irritated, and inflamed, look like purple grapes, are very painful to the touch, and bleed extensively when they burst. There are also Kapha hemorrhoids which look like green grapes, do not bleed, and are generally not problematic.
diet_plan: High-fiber diet; Vata or Pitta-pacifying based on type; keep stools soft.
foods_to_take: Psyllium husks (sat isabgol) with warm milk, warm milk with ghee, ripe bananas, cooked apples, warm water, prunes, papaya, pomegranate juice, cranberry juice
foods_to_avoid: Nightshades (potatoes, tomatoes, eggplant), spicy and fermented foods, fried foods, alcohol, red meat, hard stools-causing foods, refined flour
lifestyle_routine: For Vata type — sit on warm castor oil or sesame oil pack; take Triphala Guggulu 1 tablet 3x/day; keep stools soft with psyllium husks + warm milk at night; take Triphala ½–1 tsp at bedtime; after each bowel movement wash anal area with warm water instead of dry paper then apply castor oil; for Pitta type — apply coconut oil locally to control inflammation; drink cranberry and pomegranate juice (50:50) between meals to stop bleeding; apply turmeric + ghee mixture on hemorrhoid at bedtime; all types — drink aloe vera juice ½ cup 3x/day; drink 1 cup carrot juice + 2 tsp cilantro juice twice daily on empty stomach; avoid vigorous cycling
recommended_exercises: Leg Lift; Chest-Knee pose; Mula Bandha (root lock); gentle walking; avoid vigorous cycling and heavy lifting

name: Colitis
description: Colitis is caused when Vata pushes Pitta into the colon and inflammation occurs. Ulcerative colitis is characterized by diarrhea, mucus, and blood from the rectum. The basic line of treatment is to pacify Pitta, heal the ulcerated colon wall, and correct the underlying Vata-Pitta imbalance. The condition requires careful management and in serious cases needs medical supervision.
diet_plan: Strictly follow Pitta-pacifying diet; bland, cooling, easy-to-digest foods only.
foods_to_take: Cooked basmati rice with ghee, mung dal soup, coconut water, pomegranate juice, aloe vera gel (1 tbsp twice daily), cooked apples, fresh yogurt (room temperature)
foods_to_avoid: Spicy hot foods, fermented foods, fried foods, red meat, alcohol, raw vegetables, sour fruits, coffee, cold drinks, dairy in excess
lifestyle_routine: Do healing enema using licorice root tea (1 tbsp licorice in 1 pint water + 2 tbsp ghee) once or twice a week; do soothing coconut oil enema (1 cup slightly warm oil, retain 5 minutes); eat cooked apple pulp with ghee and nutmeg; drink apple juice for burning sensation; strictly avoid hot spicy food, alcohol, and tobacco as these severely provoke Pitta; complete rest during flare-ups; medical supervision required for ulcerative colitis; Pitta-pacifying lifestyle strictly
recommended_exercises: Leg Lift (to strengthen colon wall — hold 45 degrees for a few seconds building to 1 minute); Chest-Knee pose; Plow pose; Locust pose; Elevated Lotus; draw abdomen gradually in and out to strengthen colon wall; no vigorous exercise during flare-ups

name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome
description: According to Ayurveda, irritable bowel syndrome is due to Vata pushing Pitta into the colon. This creates alternating constipation and diarrhea, cramping, bloating, and abdominal discomfort. The aggravated Vata causes spasms and irregularity in colon movement, while the pushed Pitta creates the inflammatory and irritating component of the condition.
diet_plan: Bland easily digestible Vata-Pitta-pacifying foods; regular meal timing; no emotional eating.
foods_to_take: Well-cooked basmati rice, mung dal soup, cooked vegetables (not raw), warm water, buttermilk with roasted cumin, aloe vera juice, ginger tea
foods_to_avoid: Raw vegetables, beans and legumes, cold foods and drinks, spicy and fermented foods, fried foods, red meat, alcohol, coffee, excess dairy, gluten in some cases
lifestyle_routine: Take psyllium husks (sat isabgol) 1 tsp with ½ cup fresh yogurt 1 hour after dinner; boil flaxseed 1 tsp in water and drink at bedtime; in chronic cases introduce ½–1 cup warm sesame oil into rectum as enema (retain 5 minutes) once or twice a week to lubricate colon; maintain regular meal timing; avoid emotional eating; manage stress through meditation; identify and eliminate food triggers; avoid NSAID medications
recommended_exercises: Pavanamuktasana (Wind-Relieving pose); Nauli (abdominal churning); gentle Spinal Twist; Anulom Vilom Pranayama; stress reduction through So-Hum Meditation; gentle 20–30 minute daily walks

name: Heartburn and Acid Stomach
description: Heartburn and acid stomach are conditions of excess Pitta in the stomach causing increased acid secretion that irritates the gastric mucous membrane. Strictly following the Pitta-pacifying diet is the primary treatment. These conditions are generally quite easy to control with Ayurvedic home remedies and do not always require strong medications.
diet_plan: Strictly follow Pitta-pacifying diet; avoid all hot spicy food and overeating.
foods_to_take: Aloe vera gel (2 tbsp with pinch baking soda), papaya juice with organic sugar and cardamom, coconut water, cool sweet milk, mung dal soup, rice, banana, pomegranate juice
foods_to_avoid: Hot spicy foods, pickles, fermented food, citrus fruits, alcohol, coffee, chocolate, tomatoes, sour fruit, excessive eating, lying down after meals
lifestyle_routine: Take aloe vera gel 2 tbsp + pinch baking soda for immediate relief; use lime juice-sugar-baking soda effervescent drink in water; strictly follow Pitta-pacifying diet — no hot spicy food, no pickles, no fermented food, minimize citrus; avoid overeating; do not lie down immediately after eating; eat small frequent meals; if accompanied by chest pain radiating to neck, dizziness, or shortness of breath — seek immediate medical help (could be heart attack); practice Shitali Pranayama regularly
recommended_exercises: Shitali Pranayama (cooling breath — both cools Pitta and stimulates digestion); Shavasana after meals; gentle walking after meals; avoid vigorous exercise immediately after eating; avoid inverted poses

name: Gallstones
description: Gallstones are a Kapha disorder associated with underactive thyroid and slow metabolism. Gallstones begin with stagnation of bile in the gallbladder. The bile becomes thick, accumulates, coagulates, and slowly leads to stone formation. In general, gallstones are not painful while they remain in the gallbladder. Pain occurs only when the gallbladder tries to push the stones out through the bile duct.
diet_plan: Cooling light diet; strictly avoid fatty and spicy foods; regular meal schedule.
foods_to_take: Apple juice, warm water with lemon, light vegetarian soups, aloe vera juice, beet juice, pear juice, warm herbal teas, mung dal, pomegranate juice
foods_to_avoid: Fatty and fried foods, red meat, egg yolks, high-fat dairy, alcohol, refined sugar, hot spicy foods, chocolate, skipping meals (concentrates bile)
lifestyle_routine: Apply warm castor oil pack on abdomen above gallbladder during pain (right side, above navel below ribs) — use hot water bottle on top; for acute attack take Musta-Trikatu-Guduchi formula ¼ tsp 2–3x/day with honey; liver flush (ONLY with physician approval — small stones only): drink 8 oz olive oil + 4 oz lemon juice + chopped garlic + ¼ tsp cayenne at 6am on empty stomach, eat nothing till noon; take Triphala ½ tsp that night; avoid skipping meals which concentrates bile; maintain regular meal schedule; consult physician before any flushing treatment — dangerous if stone is large
recommended_exercises: Bow pose; Peacock pose; Spinal Twist; Narayan pose (lying on left side) — all help empty gallbladder and improve circulation preventing crystallization; Shitali Pranayama; avoid strenuous exercise during acute gallstone attack

name: Nausea and Vomiting
description: Nausea and vomiting have numerous possible causes including excess acid secretion, toxins in the liver, pregnancy, worms in the colon, food poisoning, and flu. Nausea and vomiting indicate high Pitta in the stomach with increased acid secretion irritating the gastric mucous membrane. In the event of food poisoning or excess acid secretion, vomiting occurs as a protective response of the body to eliminate toxins.
diet_plan: Anti-inflammatory Pitta-Vata-pacifying diet; keep colon clean.
foods_to_take: Pineapple juice with ginger and black pepper (3x during fast), cranberry juice, pomegranate juice, ginger tea with honey, cumin-nutmeg tea, sugar cane juice, cardamom seeds to chew, lime juice with sugar and baking soda in water
foods_to_avoid: Hot spicy food, fermented food, heavy oily foods, alcohol, dairy in excess, strong smells, cold drinks
lifestyle_routine: Fast for one day drinking pineapple juice with ginger, black pepper, and organic sugar 3x/day; or drink cranberry or pomegranate juice during fast; press 1 tablespoon ginger juice with 1 tsp onion juice; chew 1–2 cardamom seeds; mix lemon juice and honey equally, lick from finger slowly; sip 1 tsp honey + 2 pinches cardamom in half cup yogurt; drink cumin-nutmeg tea; follow Pitta-soothing diet — avoid hot spicy and fermented foods; rest lying down; avoid strong smells
recommended_exercises: Shavasana (complete rest); Shitali Pranayama when able; gentle Anulom Vilom when stable; no vigorous exercise during acute nausea; resume gentle activity after recovery

name: Ulcers
description: Ulcers in the gastrointestinal tract are a Pitta disorder. Pitta is hot, sharp, and penetrating, and when aggravated it can erode the surface of internal organs or soft tissues. The mucous membrane lining the esophagus, stomach, duodenum, or colon can develop ulceration because of excess Pitta. Individuals with a Pitta constitution, blood type O and Rh-positive, or those who take aspirin or steroids are more prone to developing ulcers.
diet_plan: Strictly follow Pitta-pacifying diet; small frequent meals; do not keep stomach empty long.
foods_to_take: Aloe vera juice, cool milk, rice, banana, cooked apples, licorice root tea (Yashti Madhu), pomegranate juice, psyllium husks (sat isabgol) with warm milk at bedtime, small regular meals
foods_to_avoid: Hot spicy foods, fermented and sour foods, citrus fruits, alcohol, tobacco, coffee, aspirin and steroids, late-night eating, long gaps between meals
lifestyle_routine: Strictly follow Pitta-pacifying diet — avoid hot spicy food, fermented food, sour fruit, citrus, alcohol, tobacco, coffee; do not take aspirin or steroids (accelerate gastric erosion); eat early in evening and eat small frequent meals — do not keep stomach empty for long; take Tranquillity Tea (Jatamamsi-Brahmi-Shanka Pushpi) 1 hour before bedtime to reduce stress; take psyllium husks (sat isabgol) 1 tsp with warm milk at bedtime; practice breathing exercises to reduce stress; drink acid-blocker herbal tea (Brahmi-Jatamamsi-Yashti Madhu) after lunch and dinner; practice Moon Breathing (left nostril only, 5–10 minutes) for cooling effect
recommended_exercises: Leg Lift; Camel pose; Cobra pose; Bow pose; Bridge pose; Spinal Twist; Shitali Pranayama; Moon Breathing (left nostril only) for cooling; avoid inverted poses and strenuous exercise

name: Dry Cough
description: Dry cough is a Vata-Kapha condition in which the respiratory tract becomes dry and irritated. The dry quality of Vata combined with Kapha blockage creates persistent bronchial spasm without productive mucus. It is often worse at night, in dry weather, and in cold environments. The treatment aims to lubricate and soothe the respiratory passage while pacifying the aggravated doshas.
diet_plan: Warm, moist, Vata-Kapha-pacifying foods; avoid dry and cold foods.
foods_to_take: Honey with black pepper (1 tsp honey + pinch black pepper), ginger-cinnamon-clove tea, licorice root tea, warm milk with turmeric and ghee, tulsi tea with honey, bay leaf-pippali in honey
foods_to_avoid: Cold and iced drinks, cold foods, ice cream, excess dairy (Kapha type), dry foods, alcohol, cigarettes, dusty environments
lifestyle_routine: Take 1 tsp honey + pinch black pepper 2–3x/day (heating quality of pepper relieves congestion); apply warm mustard oil on chest and throat; do steam inhalation; take bay leaf-pippali in 1 tsp honey 2–3x/day; take ginger tea with clove and cinnamon; drink 1 cup hot water with 1 tsp honey on empty stomach each morning; avoid cold and dusty environments; avoid smoking and secondhand smoke; keep body warm at all times; avoid air conditioning directly on body
recommended_exercises: Kapalabhati Pranayama (gentle); Anulom Vilom Pranayama; Cobra pose (Bhujangasana); Fish pose (Matsyasana); avoid exercise in cold or dusty air; avoid vigorous cardio that dries the respiratory tract further

name: Productive Cough
description: Productive cough with mucus results from excess Kapha accumulation in the respiratory tract. The Kapha blocks the natural flow of air, creating congestion, mucus production, and cough as the body attempts to clear the passages. Mucus may be white when pure Kapha is involved, or yellow-green when there is secondary Pitta infection. Dairy products, cold foods, and sweet heavy foods are the primary aggravating factors.
diet_plan: Light, dry, warm, Kapha-reducing foods; strictly avoid mucus-forming foods.
foods_to_take: Mustard seed tea with pippali and honey, onion juice (¼ cup) with honey and black pepper, spinach juice with pippali, cinnamon-trikatu tea with honey, warm water, ginger tea, pomegranate juice
foods_to_avoid: All dairy products (milk, cheese, yogurt), cold food and drinks, fried foods, sweets, bananas, excess wheat, fermented foods, alcohol, cold beverages
lifestyle_routine: Take Sitopaladi churna ¼ tsp with honey 3x/day; for yellow-green mucus (infection) use Sitopaladi-Maha Sudarshan tea ¼ tsp 3x/day with honey; do Nasya with warm ghee (5 drops in each nostril); steam inhalation with eucalyptus oil; apply mustard oil chest massage; for chronic bronchial asthma insert 7 cloves in peeled banana overnight, eat next morning, then 1 cup hot water + 1 tsp honey after 1 hour; follow strictly Kapha-reducing lifestyle; avoid dairy, cold, and damp environments
recommended_exercises: Kapalabhati Pranayama; Bhastrika (Breath of Fire); Bow pose; Cobra pose; Vajrasana (sitting on heels); Shoulder Stand; Plow pose; inverted poses to drain mucus from respiratory tract

name: Sinus Problems
description: The sinuses are air-filled cavities located on either side of the nose, covered with a superfine mucous membrane whose chief function is to keep the nasal cavity moist. Due to allergies, colds, or bacterial infections, the sinuses sometimes get clogged or infected. This is a condition of excess Kapha aggravated by cold drinks, dairy products, and smoking. Sinus problems can create complications from headaches and difficulty breathing to ear infections and in serious cases meningitis.
diet_plan: Light, warm, dry, Kapha-Vata-pacifying diet; avoid mucus-forming foods strictly.
foods_to_take: Fresh ginger juice with honey (2-3x daily), warm water, spiced soups with black pepper and turmeric, ginger tea, pomegranate juice, hot herbal teas, garlic in cooking
foods_to_avoid: Dairy products especially cheese, yogurt, and ice cream; cold drinks; cold weather exposure; smoking; wheat in excess; bananas; sweet heavy foods
lifestyle_routine: Take fresh ginger juice + 1 tsp honey 2–3x/day as herbal decongestant; take Sitopaladi-Trikatu-Maha Sudarshan formula ¼ tsp 2–3x/day; do nasal wash — dissolve ½ tsp salt in ½ cup lukewarm water, instill 5 drops in each nostril several times daily; medicated steam inhalation — add 3–5 drops eucalyptus oil or boil fresh ginger in water, inhale steam with towel over head; for severe congestion insert a few drops fresh garlic juice in each nostril (tilt head back 5 minutes) — up to 3x/day for severe attack; instill warm ghee in each nostril daily for prevention; avoid dairy, cold drinks, cold weather, smoking
recommended_exercises: Vigorous Kapalabhati Pranayama; Bhastrika (Breath of Fire); Sun Salutation; brisk walking; Jalaneti (nasal irrigation) under expert guidance; avoid sleeping after sunrise

name: Allergies (Kapha-Type)
description: Kapha-type allergies are experienced primarily during spring season when plants and trees shed pollens into the atmosphere. When pollens are inhaled they enter the nasal-respiratory passage and in some people irritate the delicate mucous membrane, leading to hay fever, colds, congestion, cough, sinus infection, and even asthma. The excess Kapha in the respiratory tract is the primary mechanism, and treatment focuses on reducing Kapha and strengthening immunity.
diet_plan: Light, dry, warm, Kapha-reducing diet during allergy season.
foods_to_take: Ginger tea, tulsi tea, honey with warm water, spiced light soups, pomegranate juice, bitter vegetables, warming spices (black pepper, ginger, turmeric), triphala tea
foods_to_avoid: Dairy products, cold food and drinks, wheat, sweets, bananas, heavy oily foods, fermented foods, refrigerated food, excess salt
lifestyle_routine: Take Sitopaladi-Yashti Madhu-Abrak Bhasma formula ¼ tsp 3x/day with honey; lubricate nasal mucous membrane with ghee before going out to block allergens; apply neem oil on exposed skin to minimize allergen contact; take Triphala ½–1 tsp at night (works for all three types of allergies); avoid known allergens; avoid synthetic fibers — wear cotton clothing; do vomiting therapy (Vamana) only if able — drink licorice tea + salt water to fill stomach, then vomit out to remove excess Kapha from lungs (not for hypertension, hiatal hernia, or heart problems); avoid pollen-heavy environments during spring
recommended_exercises: Sun Salutation (Vata and Kapha types); Alternate Nostril Breathing for respiratory allergies; Bhastrika (Breath of Fire) for Kapha-type congestive allergies; Ujjayi Pranayama to improve immunity; Kapalabhati Pranayama

name: Sore Throat
description: A sore throat is caused by irritation and inflammation of the throat from Pitta-Kapha imbalance. This condition is generally quite easy to remedy using Ayurvedic methods. The inflammation can be of viral or bacterial origin, and the hot, sharp quality of Pitta combined with Kapha congestion creates the characteristic pain and difficulty swallowing. It commonly accompanies colds, coughs, and respiratory infections.
diet_plan: Soothing warm liquid diet; avoid cold and spicy foods.
foods_to_take: Warm milk boiled with ½ tsp turmeric, ginger-cinnamon-licorice tea (up to 3x/day), warm water with honey, tulsi-ginger tea, warm soups, honey, pomegranate juice
foods_to_avoid: Cold dairy (cheese, yogurt, ice cream), fermented foods, cold drinks, spicy foods, fried foods, sour foods, alcohol, cigarettes
lifestyle_routine: Gargle with 1 cup hot water + ½ tsp turmeric + ½ tsp salt, morning and evening; drink 1 cup hot milk boiled with ½ tsp turmeric; drink ginger-cinnamon-licorice tea (2:2:3 ratio) up to 3x/day; do Brahmi ghee Nasya to soothe throat from above; steam inhalation; rest the voice; avoid chilled dairy products, fermented foods, cold drinks, cigarettes; warm milk exception is fine but no cold dairy; see doctor if persists beyond a few days
recommended_exercises: Simhasana (Lion Pose) — traditionally recommended for sore throats; Yoga Mudra; Bhramari (Humming Breath) Pranayama; gentle Anulom Vilom Pranayama; Shavasana; avoid vigorous exercise and shouting

name: Backache
description: Backache has become an occupational hazard in many different kinds of work. People may pull or strain their back muscles while lifting a heavy load or sitting at a desk for long hours. Emotional factors can also lead to back pain, as can injuries from accidents. Some people may have a slipped disk causing severe back pain. According to Ayurveda, backache is primarily a Vata disorder — the dry, cold, mobile qualities of Vata cause muscle stiffness, spasm, and pain in the lower back region.
diet_plan: Follow Vata-pacifying diet; avoid ata-aggravating foods.
foods_to_take: Warm cooked foods, ghee, sesame seeds, warm milk, ginger tea, well-cooked root vegetables, Musta herb tea (¼ tsp 2-3x/day), dashamoola tea
foods_to_avoid: Most beans (black, pinto, adzuki, garbanzo), raw cold salads, cold beverages, excess caffeine, carbonated drinks, exposure to cold wind
lifestyle_routine: Rub Mahanarayan oil on painful back area (Vata and Pitta — rub surface only; Kapha — deeper massage); apply ginger powder paste on area for 10–15 minutes then rub with eucalyptus oil; take hot ginger-baking soda bath (⅓ cup each) for 10–15 minutes, 2–3x/week (Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday); do Dashamoola-sesame oil enema if constipation is a factor (1 tbsp dashamoola in 1 pint water + ½ cup sesame oil, retain 5–10 minutes); sit quietly and meditate to relax tense muscles; avoid high-heeled shoes; avoid jogging, jumping, and strenuous exercise; avoid cold weather and cold winds; minimize sexual activity; do not do yoga without trained teacher guidance when in pain
recommended_exercises: Camel pose; Lotus pose; Cow pose; Forward Bend; Spinal Twist; Palm Tree pose; Locust pose; gentle modified Fish pose — ALL must be learned and performed under trained yoga teacher guidance especially if slipped disk is involved

name: Migraine
description: Although migraine headaches can result from Vata, Pitta, or Kapha imbalance, they most frequently occur when systemic Pitta moves into the cardiovascular system, circulates, and affects the blood vessels around the brain. The hot, sharp quality of Pitta dilates the blood vessels and creates pressure on the nerves, causing this painful condition. Migraines are worsened by bright sun, hot weather, skipping meals, and Pitta-provoking foods.
diet_plan: Vital to follow Pitta-soothing diet; especially avoid hot spicy and fermented foods.
foods_to_take: Ripe banana with warm ghee, date sugar and cardamom (preventive breakfast), shatavari-brahmi herbal tea, coconut water, sweet fruits, pomegranate juice, cool milk, cucumber
foods_to_avoid: Hot spicy foods, fermented foods, sour and citrus fruits, alcohol, red meat, direct sun exposure, skipping meals, dehydration, pickles
lifestyle_routine: Follow Pitta-soothing diet strictly — most important preventive step; preventive breakfast each morning: 1 ripe banana chopped + 1 tsp warm ghee + 1 tsp date sugar + pinch cardamom; take Shatavari-Brahmi-Jatamamsi-Musta formula ½ tsp twice daily after breakfast and dinner; put 5 drops warm Brahmi ghee in each nostril (Nasya) once headache develops; avoid direct sun exposure — wear a hat outdoors; gently squeeze earlobes pulling ear down and yawn to relieve pressure on blood vessels; rest in cool dark room during attack; apply sandalwood paste on forehead and temples
recommended_exercises: Moon Salutation; Hidden Lotus pose; Boat pose; Bow pose; Spinal Twist; Palm Tree pose; Standing on Toes; Shitali Pranayama (cooling breath); avoid Sun Salutation and heating practices; avoid exercise during acute attack

name: Kapha-Type Headache
description: Kapha headaches occur because of a Kapha-producing diet that causes systemic Kapha to increase in the stomach, enter the general circulation, and lodge in the sinuses. This creates dull, heavy, frontal sinus headaches that are worse in the morning and associated with congestion, mucus, and sluggishness. They tend to worsen in cold damp weather and improve as the day progresses and the person becomes more active.
diet_plan: Follow Kapha-reducing diet; light, warm, dry, spiced foods.
foods_to_take: Ginger tea, hot water with honey and lime, spiced foods with black pepper and turmeric, light warm soups, mustard seed tea, pomegranate juice, sitopaladi-trikatu with honey
foods_to_avoid: Dairy products, cold food and drinks, wheat, sweets, fried foods, heavy oily foods, alcohol, excess salt, bananas, sleeping in the morning
lifestyle_routine: Apply dry ginger powder paste on forehead; steam inhalation with eucalyptus oil to clear congestion; take Sitopaladi-Trikatu formula with honey; Nasya with warm Brahmi ghee or plain ghee in each nostril; vigorous Kapalabhati to clear sinus congestion; avoid sleeping after sunrise — daytime sleep greatly increases Kapha; do not eat dairy, cold foods, or sweets; stay active throughout the day; avoid cold and damp environments; garlic juice drops in nostrils for severe sinus congestion if headache is sinus-related
recommended_exercises: Vigorous Kapalabhati Pranayama; Bhastrika (Breath of Fire); Sun Salutation; brisk walking; avoid morning sleep and sedentary lifestyle which aggravates Kapha

name: Muscle Cramps and Spasms
description: Muscle cramps and spasms have numerous causes. They may be due to insufficient blood supply to the muscle, excess blood supply as in writer's cramp, lack of calcium in the diet, malabsorption of calcium, underactive parathyroid, or exposure to cold. The Ayurvedic understanding is that muscle spasms are caused by Vata dosha. Vata, increased by its mobile, cold, or rough qualities, makes a muscle stiff and hard, causing it to undergo spasm.
diet_plan: Vata-pacifying nourishing diet; adequate minerals especially calcium, magnesium, and zinc.
foods_to_take: Warm milk with sesame seeds, bananas, warm soups with ghee, sesame seeds, almonds, cooked root vegetables, warm water, chamomile tea, dashamoola tea (prevention)
foods_to_avoid: Cold foods and drinks, excess caffeine, alcohol, beans, carbonated beverages, raw salads, dry foods, exposure to cold weather
lifestyle_routine: For immediate relief — grab hold of the cramping muscle, press index finger deeply into the belly (center) of the muscle for 15–20 seconds at the marma point; take deep breaths; for upper body spasm grab upper lip at midpoint between thumb and index finger for 30 seconds; for lower body spasm grab lower lip; repeatedly alternate contraction and relaxation of cramped muscle; apply Mahanarayan oil or sesame oil on muscle belly and massage gently then apply hot water bottle; soak cramped foot in bucket of warm salt water (1–2 tbsp salt); take hot ginger-baking soda bath (⅔ cup baking soda + ¼ cup ginger per tub) for full body muscle relaxation; take calcium 1200mg + magnesium 600mg + zinc 60mg daily supplements; drink dashamoola tea preventively every Saturday
recommended_exercises: Gentle stretching; Forward Bend; Camel pose; Anulom Vilom Pranayama; gentle yoga; contract and relax exercises for affected muscles; avoid overstretching during active spasm

name: Bursitis
description: Bursitis is an inflammation of the bursae — the small fluid sacs around the shoulder, knee, and other parts of the body. According to Ayurveda, bursitis is a Pitta condition that is similar to arthritis. The pathological process begins with toxicity accumulated in the colon. These toxins get absorbed into the bloodstream, go into the general circulation, and lodge in the bursae, leading to the symptoms of bursitis. Treatment is similar to the treatment for Pitta-type arthritis.
diet_plan: Anti-inflammatory Pitta-Vata-pacifying diet; keep colon clean.
foods_to_take: Coconut water, cucumber, coriander tea, aloe vera gel, turmeric in food, ginger tea, cooling sweet fruits, pomegranate juice, mung dal soup
foods_to_avoid: Hot spicy foods, fermented foods, alcohol, red meat, sour foods, excess salt, heavy dairy, repetitive aggravating movements
lifestyle_routine: Take Kaishore Guggulu 1 tablet 3x/day; apply sandalwood paste gently on painful area; do Nasya with warm ghee (5 drops in each nostril) to open Prana flow through connective tissue; apply sesame oil, eucalyptus oil, tea tree oil, Mahanarayan oil, or neem oil topically on swollen joint; take 1 cup hot milk + 2 tsp castor oil at bedtime to eliminate Pitta toxicity from colon (adjust dose to get 2 good bowel movements); or take Triphala or Amalaki 1 tsp in warm water at night; strictly follow Pitta-soothing diet — avoid hot spicy foods, fermented foods, raw vegetables, salad, ice water, cold drinks, beans; do not do strenuous exercise while bursitis is present
recommended_exercises: Gentle yoga stretching only; Camel pose; Cobra pose; Cow pose; Cat pose; Spinal Twist; Forward Bend — all under guidance of a yoga teacher; Shitali Pranayama; Shavasana; NO strenuous exercise during active bursitis

name: Diabetes
description: Diabetes is a metabolic Kapha-type disorder in which diminished functioning of Agni (digestive fire) leads to a tendency toward high blood sugar. The weakened cellular fire cannot burn the excess food consumed, which then gets converted to excess sugar in the blood. Clinical observation in Ayurveda suggests that insulin-dependent diabetics who follow Ayurvedic treatment often experience a markedly diminished requirement for insulin as the condition comes under better control.
diet_plan: Follow Kapha-pacifying diet; especially avoid excess sweets, carbohydrates, and dairy.
foods_to_take: Bitter vegetables, bitter gourd, fenugreek seeds, turmeric capsules (2 caps 3x/day before meals), ground bay leaf with turmeric in aloe vera gel, copper water (stored overnight in copper vessel), fresh green vegetables
foods_to_avoid: Excess sweets, refined carbohydrates, dairy products, white rice, white bread, fried foods, alcohol, cold drinks, fruit juices, processed foods
lifestyle_routine: ake Guduchi-Shardunika-Kutki-Punarnava formula ½ tsp 2–3x/day with warm water; take turmeric capsules (2 capsules 3x/day) a few minutes before meals for up to 1 month; take ½ tsp ground bay leaf + ½ tsp turmeric in 1 tbsp aloe vera gel twice daily before lunch and dinner; drink copper water each morning (water stored overnight in copper vessel); follow Kapha-pacifying diet — avoid excess sweets, carbohydrates, and dairy; take more bitter herbs and fresh vegetables; monitor blood sugar regularly; consult physician — insulin-dependent patients may experience markedly diminished insulin requirement with Ayurvedic treatment
recommended_exercises: Sun Salutation (daily); Peacock pose; Locust pose; Leg Lift; Chest-Knee pose; Alternate Nostril Breathing (Anulom Vilom) — these strengthen the pancreas and improve glucose metabolism

name: Obesity
description: Obesity is a condition in which an excessive amount of body fat has accumulated under the chin, on the breasts, belly, buttocks, and thighs. Obesity is a Kapha disorder. In obese individuals the gastric fire is strong, but the cellular fire in the tissue is relatively low. Whatever excess food or calories a person consumes are not burned and instead turn into adipose tissue. The main causes according to Ayurveda are eating too much, sitting too much, and doing too little.
diet_plan: Follow Kapha-pacifying diet; skip breakfast or eat biggest meal at noon; light supper.
foods_to_take: Hot water with herbal teas (ginger, mint, cinnamon), salad without creamy dressings, beans, fish or chicken (once a month maximum), bitter vegetables, light grains
foods_to_avoid: Cold drinks, fatty fried foods, dairy (cheese, yogurt, ice cream), excess sugar and carbohydrates, alcohol, tobacco, beef, lamb, pork, snacks between meals, late-night eating
lifestyle_routine: Skip breakfast entirely (have hot herbal tea only) and take biggest meal at noon; supper should be light; do not snack between meals; drink hot water or herbal teas (ginger, mint, cinnamon) instead of cold drinks always; take hot water with 1 tsp honey each morning; quit alcohol and smoking; avoid cold drinks immediately after exercise (slows metabolism); lift weights using 5-pound weights to burn adipose tissue; follow Kapha-pacifying diet strictly; take biggest meal early in day; eat 2 meals only when possible — skip supper; burn more calories than you consume
recommended_exercises: Daily walk of at least half an hour — essential; gentle jogging (walk fast with 2.5-pound hand weights if unable to jog); swimming; aerobic exercise at least 3x/week; weight lifting with 5-pound weights; Vigorous Kapalabhati Pranayama; Sun Salutation (12+ cycles daily); Uddiyana Bandha (abdominal lock)

name: High Cholesterol
description: High cholesterol means increased lipids (fats) in the blood. It is essentially a metabolic disorder. People with low liver function, diminished thyroid activity, those who have taken steroids in the past, or whose diet is very Kapha-generating seem most prone to develop high cholesterol. A cholesterol level of 200 or above is worrisome as it tends to create plaque on artery walls, resulting in atherosclerotic changes, cardiovascular disorders, hypertension, and stroke.
diet_plan: Stick to Kapha-pacifying diet; no fatty fried food; no high-fat dairy.
foods_to_take: Raw garlic (1 clove daily, chopped fine), ginger tea, oats, barley, artichoke, bitter gourd, fenugreek seeds, turmeric, apple cider vinegar with honey in warm water, onion in cooking
foods_to_avoid: Fatty meats, fried foods, cheese, high-fat milk and yogurt, sweets, cold food and drinks, excessive carbohydrates, alcohol, smoking
lifestyle_routine: Eat 1 clove fresh garlic (chopped fine) + ½ tsp grated ginger + ½ tsp lime juice before each meal; drink cinnamon-trikatu tea (1 tsp cinnamon + ¼ tsp trikatu steeped 10 minutes + 1 tsp honey) twice daily; take Trikatu ½ tsp with 1 tsp honey 2–3x/day; take Kutki-Chitrak-Shilajit formula ½ tsp twice daily with honey and hot water; take Triphala Guggulu 1 tablet (200mg) 3x/day; take Chitrak-Adhivati 1 tablet (200mg) twice daily after lunch and dinner; drink hot water with 1 tsp honey + lime juice or apple cider vinegar each morning to "scrape" fat from system; walk Monday to Friday at least half an hour daily; swim at least 3x/week
recommended_exercises: Sun Salutation (daily); Shoulder Stand; Peacock pose; Cobra pose; Spinal Twist; Locust pose; Lotus pose; Bhastrika (Breath of Fire) Pranayama; half-hour daily walk Monday to Friday; swimming 3x/week

name: Edema
description: Any part of the body can suddenly swell. The edema may be related to an injury, torn ligaments, or fluid seeping from blood vessels due to prolonged standing or walking. Poor circulation can cause feet or ankles to swell. Edema may also be an allergic reaction or the result of an insect bite. Because there are many possible reasons for swelling, finding out the cause is essential for effective treatment. In Ayurveda, edema is primarily a Kapha disorder involving excess fluid retention.
diet_plan: Light, dry, low-salt diet; address the underlying cause.
foods_to_take: Barley water, ginger tea, pomegranate juice, cranberry juice, coconut water, cilantro juice (natural antihistamine if allergy-related), warm low-sodium soups, pineapple juice (anti-inflammatory)
foods_to_avoid: Salt in excess, dairy products, cold food and drinks, heavy meats, wheat, sweets, fried foods, alcohol, processed canned foods
lifestyle_routine: Apply turmeric + red sandalwood paste at the site of swelling; elevate feet — sit with legs on footstool, place pillows under feet when in bed; for insect bite edema apply neem + tea tree oil equally; for allergy edema drink fresh cilantro juice and apply pulp locally; for finger ring swelling raise hand above head or soak in ice water then use soap to slide off ring; take Punarnava-Manjistha-Gokshura circulation formula (3 tsp in warm water 2–3x/day); reduce salt drastically; avoid prolonged standing or sitting; avoid dairy, heavy foods, sweets; light massage toward heart (not away) to promote lymphatic drainage
recommended_exercises: Gentle walking; Leg Lift (legs at 45-degree angle, hold a few seconds); elevate legs above heart level while resting; Sun Salutation; Kapalabhati Pranayama; Anulom Vilom; avoid vigorous exercise during acute swelling

name: Chronic Fatigue
description: Fatigue may be due to low gastric fire, weakness of the liver, low adrenal energy, or anemia. Chronic fatigue syndrome may be caused by Epstein-Barr virus, a form related to high stagnant Pitta in the liver. People with a history of infectious mononucleosis can feel very tired for extended periods. Importantly, fatigue is not always due to overwork — sometimes people feel tired because they are not doing enough and lack motivation, which is a Kapha-type fatigue.
diet_plan: Strengthening diet based on cause; Pitta-pacifying if Epstein-Barr related; blood-building if anemia related.
foods_to_take: Fresh orange juice with rock salt (after exercise fatigue), ginseng or ashwagandha tea, pomegranate juice, grapes, beet-carrot juice, shatavari-bala-vidari formula with shatavari ghee, warm milk with saffron
foods_to_avoid: Alcohol and drugs (destroy brain and liver cells), heavy red meat, excessive sugar, processed foods, junk food, excess caffeine, cold drinks, overworking without rest
lifestyle_routine: For Epstein-Barr/chronic fatigue take Shatavari-Bala-Vidari-Kama Dudha formula ½ tsp 2–3x/day with 1 tsp shatavari ghee; to strengthen Agni — chew fresh ginger with lime juice and salt before each meal; avoid cold and iced drinks especially during and after meals; take Chitrak-Adivati 1 tablet (200mg) twice daily after lunch and dinner to kindle Agni; energy-building foods — soak 10 fresh dates in ghee with ginger, cardamom, saffron for 2 weeks; eat 1 date each morning; eat 1 ripe mango daily followed by warm milk + 1 tsp ghee after an hour; unless fatigue is due to idleness — do NOT do much exercise as it burns Ojas and increases fatigue
recommended_exercises: Alternate Nostril Breathing (Anulom Vilom) to kindle gastric fire; gentle yoga stretching only — NOT vigorous exercise; short gentle walking; Shavasana (rest is therapeutic); NOTE: for Kapha-type fatigue from idleness — DO exercise vigorously to break inertia

name: Anxiety
description: Anxiety, which is often associated with insomnia and feelings of fear, is due primarily to aggravation of Vata dosha in the nervous system. The light, mobile, and irregular qualities of Vata when disturbed create excessive mental activity, worry, and fear. To heal anxiety, Ayurveda focuses on balancing Vata through warming, grounding, and nourishing practices. If severe anxiety continues for a long time or includes panic attacks and interferes with daily functioning, medical attention is required.
diet_plan: Warm, grounding, nourishing Vata-pacifying foods; avoid all stimulants.
foods_to_take: Warm almond milk with nutmeg and saffron (10 almonds soaked overnight, blended in warm milk), orange juice with 1 tsp honey and pinch nutmeg (for fast heart rate), chamomile tea, warm milk, sweet fruits, cooked root vegetables, ghee
foods_to_avoid: Coffee, tea, cola, alcohol, cold drinks, spicy foods, raw salads, beans, excessive fasting, carbonated drinks
lifestyle_routine: Take warm ginger-baking soda bath (⅓ cup ginger + ⅓ cup baking soda in tub) for 10–15 minutes; drink calming Tagar-Musta tea ½ tsp twice daily; give yourself full-body oil massage before morning bath (Vata — sesame oil, Pitta — sunflower/coconut oil, Kapha — corn oil, 6–7 oz warm oil head to toe); minimal massage on scalp and soles of feet also effective; press palm pressure point — make fist, press middle finger point in center of palm firmly for 1 minute to calm Prana agitation; lie in Savasana; meditate using So-Hum meditation focusing on top of head; spend time in nature; avoid coffee, tea, cola, alcohol, and all stimulants
recommended_exercises: Savasana (Corpse pose — lie on back arms by sides); Anulom Vilom Pranayama; So-Hum Meditation; gentle walking in fresh air; gentle yoga; when urge to worry arises — go for a walk immediately

name: Depression (Vata-Type)
description: Vata-type depression is generally associated with fear, anxiety, nervousness, and insomnia. Because of specific etiological factors, Vata from the colon enters the general circulation, lodges in the nervous system, and interferes with the normal functioning of the mind, causing depression. Vata depression is characterized by feeling spacy, ungrounded, fearful, and anxious. Loneliness is one psychological factor that particularly sustains this type of depression.
diet_plan: Follow Vata-pacifying diet strictly; warm nourishing meals are essential.
foods_to_take: Dashamoola tea (1 tsp steeped in hot water twice daily), ashwagandha-brahmi tea (2-3x/day), tulsi-sage tea, warm milk with saffron, warm almond milk, ghee, sesame seeds, warm root vegetables
foods_to_avoid: Cold foods and drinks, dry foods, beans, barley, excess caffeine, alcohol, raw salads, excessive fasting, leftover food
lifestyle_routine: Do warm sesame oil Nasya (3–5 drops in each nostril) morning and evening on empty stomach; rub top of head and soles of feet with sesame oil; spend time relating to people — combat loneliness which sustains Vata depression; drink Dashamoola tea twice daily; drink Ashwagandha-Brahmi tea 2–3x/day; take Tulsi-Sage tea twice daily; maintain warm and nourishing daily routine; avoid excessive alone time; follow Vata-pacifying diet strictly; get adequate sleep with regular schedule; avoid cold environments; medical supervision is essential — these are adjunct therapies only
recommended_exercises: Sun Salutation (12 repetitions daily); Shoulder Stand; Plow pose; Maha Mudra; Bow pose; Vajrasana (Sitting on Heels); Ujjayi Pranayama; So-Hum Meditation

name: Depression (Kapha-Type)
description: Kapha-type depression enters the general circulation from the stomach and lodges in the nervous system creating heaviness, lethargy, excessive sleep, overeating, grief, and profound lack of motivation. The heavy, slow, dense qualities of Kapha cause the person to feel deeply stuck and unmotivated. Vigorous exercise, stimulating foods, and regular daily routine are especially important for managing this type of depression. Medical supervision is essential for serious cases.
diet_plan: Follow Kapha-reducing diet; light, spiced, stimulating foods; avoid heavy and sweet foods.
foods_to_take: Gotu kola or brahmi tea (½ tsp in hot water 2-3x/day), ginkgo tea, pomegranate juice, ginger tea, pungent spices (black pepper, mustard, fenugreek), light soups, honey with warm water
foods_to_avoid: Dairy products, sweets, refined sugar, fried foods, cold food and drinks, excess wheat, heavy meats, alcohol, sleeping after sunrise
lifestyle_routine: Fast for 3–4 days on apple juice to lighten heaviness of Kapha depression; increase the amount of exercise significantly; drink ginger tea (½–1 tsp ginger powder in hot water) twice daily; take Sarasvati-Punarnava-Chitrak formula ½ tsp 3x/day with warm water; apply Punarnava Ghee Nasya (5 drops in each nostril) twice daily; avoid sleeping after sunrise — daytime sleep greatly worsens Kapha depression; engage in stimulating social activities; maintain vigorous daily routine; avoid dairy, sweets, heavy foods, and alcohol; fast one day per week on apple juice; see doctor if depression is serious
recommended_exercises: Sun Salutation (12 repetitions daily); Shoulder Stand; Plow pose; Maha Mudra; Bow pose; Vajrasana; Vigorous Kapalabhati Pranayama; Bhastrika (Breath of Fire); brisk walking; Ujjayi Pranayama; avoid sedentary lifestyle completely

name: Memory Problems
description: Memory problems are often the result of an inadequate supply of nutrients to the brain. As age advances, memory frequently becomes poorer. Alcohol destroys brain cells and memory loss among alcoholics is quite common. Memory problems may also be due to LSD, marijuana, cocaine, and alcohol abuse. According to Ayurvedic principles, memory is recorded on the sensitive film of nerve cells in the brain which are of Kapha nature, while memory is recalled by Vata. Most memory problems are due to stagnation of Kapha or aggravation of Vata in the brain.
diet_plan: Sattvic brain-nourishing diet; avoid heavy meat and all brain-damaging substances.
foods_to_take: Brahmi milk (½ tsp brahmi boiled in warm milk at bedtime with pinch saffron), carrot and beet juice, almonds, sweet potatoes, tapioca, okra, spinach, ghee, fresh fruits and vegetables, orange juice (for drowsiness), pomegranate juice
foods_to_avoid: Heavy red meat, alcohol, marijuana, LSD and drugs, excessive sugar, processed foods, junk food, excess caffeine, smoking (constricts cerebral blood vessels), diazepam (Valium) if possible
lifestyle_routine: Take Medhya herbs tea (Brahmi + Jatamamsi + Bhringaraj + Shanka Pushpi — equal proportions, 1 tsp in 1 cup hot water) on empty stomach morning and evening for 1 month; drink Brahmi milk (½ tsp brahmi in warm milk + pinch saffron) at bedtime; take Brahmi Ghee 1 tsp before breakfast and before dinner; do 5-day Kitchari fast periodically; do 3–5-day fruit fast with Triphala at night; rub Brahmi oil on soles of feet and scalp daily to stimulate cerebral neural receptors; Brahmi Ghee Nasya (5 drops warm Brahmi ghee in each nostril); write down important things; use rhymes, rhythms, and association techniques; avoid alcohol, marijuana, LSD, cocaine, and smoking; Regular Meditation using Empty Bowl or So-Hum; avoid diazepam (Valium) if possible
recommended_exercises: Daily fast walking 30 minutes, Monday to Friday (improves cerebral circulation); inverted poses — Shoulder Stand, Headstand (with supervision), Plow pose, Camel pose (bring blood to brain); Bow pose; Cobra pose; Savasana; Sun Salutation 12 cycles daily; Alternate Nostril Breathing (Anulom Vilom); So-Hum Meditation and Empty Bowl Meditation

name: Urinary Tract Infection
description: Urinary tract infection (Cystitis) is primarily a Pitta condition causing burning inflammation of the bladder. The hot, sharp, penetrating quality of aggravated Pitta creates the characteristic burning sensation during urination, frequent urge to urinate, and lower abdominal discomfort. The condition is aggravated by hot spicy foods, fermented foods, alcohol, and coffee. Cooling Pitta and increasing fluid intake are the foundations of Ayurvedic treatment.
diet_plan: Cooling alkaline high-fluid diet; Pitta-pacifying; increase fluid intake drastically.
foods_to_take: Coriander seed tea, cumin tea, fennel tea, cumin-coriander-fennel tea (3x/day), coconut water, barley water, cool water, pomegranate juice, cucumber, watermelon, cranberry juice
foods_to_avoid: Hot spicy foods, fermented foods, alcohol, coffee, sour citrus fruits, red meat, excess salt, chocolate, tomatoes, cold drinks that irritate bladder
lifestyle_routine: Drink Cumin-Coriander-Fennel tea 3x/day; increase fluid intake drastically to dilute urine and flush bacteria; apply cool compress on lower abdomen for relief; take Punarnava-Gokshura-Musta formula ½ tsp 2–3x/day with warm water; drink cranberry juice regularly (natural antiseptic for urinary tract); avoid holding urine — urinate when needed; keep lower abdomen warm; avoid cold drinks and cold environments; if symptoms worsen or blood appears in urine — see physician immediately; in cases of urinary incontinence take white sesame seeds + 1 tsp jaggery with ½ cup water once daily to strengthen bladder sphincter
recommended_exercises: Gentle walking; Baddha Konasana (Butterfly pose); Setu Bandhasana (Bridge pose); Anulom Vilom Pranayama; Mula Bandha (root lock) to strengthen pelvic floor; adequate hydration before and during all exercise; avoid intense exercise during acute infection
